syncMode.description:
How the watcher reacts to local file edits.
- 'auto'   (default): push changes within the debounce window; periodic full sync.
- 'manual': watcher only runs periodic pulls; pushes are user-driven via Sync Now /   Push to Ghost. Lets cautious users avoid surprise writes to their blog.

vaultPath.description: Absolute path to the vault root.

watchDebounceMs.description: Debounce window (ms) for the file watcher before flushing changes.

targets.description: Multi-target list. Always present after `loadConfig()` (synthesized from legacy fields if absent on disk). Always written to disk by `saveConfig`.

handle.description: URL-safe handle — also the folder name when multiple targets are configured.

syncFolderPath.description: Subfolder under the target's effective root. Empty means the effective root itself is used. In single-target configs, this collapses to the legacy `syncFolderPath` behavior.

TargetConfig.description:
Per-target configuration: one CMS connection + the engine-visible sync settings that govern how its content lives in the vault.

v0.4.0 Phase B introduces this as the canonical unit; legacy single-Ghost configs (every shipped user) auto-synthesize a single `targets[0]` on load via `synthesizeLegacyTarget`. The writer still emits the legacy flat fields alongside `targets[]` for one release window so a downgrade is non-fatal.

accessToken.description: Admin API access token (shpat_… for dev-store, OAuth token in prod).

refreshToken.description: OAuth refresh token for expiring offline tokens. Rotated after every refresh.

accessTokenExpiresAt.description: ISO timestamp when the access token expires.

refreshTokenExpiresAt.description: ISO timestamp when the refresh token expires.

apiVersion.description: GraphQL API version. Defaults to the adapter's pinned version.

siteUrl.description: Full URL of the WordPress site, e.g. "https://myblog.com".

AdapterConfig.description: Per-platform credential block. Discriminated by `platform`. Other config (vault path, sync folder, conflict strategy, …) stays in the top-level DaemonConfig — these fields are purely the CMS connection.
